column1: 1. A quiet kind of magic lives in ordinary moments—the clink of a spoon in a cup
column2: the pause before a thought finishes forming

column1: 2. the way sunlight chooses one corner of a room and commits to it completely.
3. Time doesn’t announce itself when it’s passing; it just leaves small fingerprints everywhere
column2: like softened edges and familiar habits.

column3: we keep becoming slightly different versions of ourselves

column1: 5. without ever noticing the exact second it happens.
